ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.

text: Yele boi ya zuo, yamam gɔnmi'ineba la Farasiiduma wa, birekɔgenduma wa. text: Yinɛ n ta nyuulena la, ti a zahereba la paaɛ a zi'an yele en yeti, ka'lam dela muuen, ti yinɛ me nyuulum.

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ti Yezu lerege ba yeti, “Yamam mɛhi bo' ba dia ti ba di.” Ti ba yele en yeti, “Tumam wan ta'am nyɛ dinaari kɔbehi siyi da' dia bo' nɛreba wa ti ba di?”

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ti a yele ba yeti, “Ya tari la boroboro ba lɛ? Kiŋɛ ya ta bihɛ.” Ti ba kiŋɛ ta bihɛ lebe na yele en yeti, “Tu tari la boroboro banuu, la zim i yi'.”

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Ti a bo' ba nuurɛ ti ba bahɛ ti nɛreba la tigehe taaba zi'ire muɔ la zuo.

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Bala ti nɛreba wa tigehe taaba kɔbega kɔbega, la pihinuu pihinuu zi'ire.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba za'a nyɛ en mɛ ti dabeem nyɔkɛ paaɛ paaɛ. Gee bilam bilam ti Yezu tɔgɛ bo' ba yeti, “Nyɔkɛ ya ya mɛhi gee la dela mam. Da zoti ya dabeem.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Ti Yezu dikɛ boroboro banuu la zim i yi' la, gorege bihɛ saazuo, purɛ sum bahɛ dia la zuo gee fige boroboro la, dikɛ bo' a zahereba la yeti, ba to'e tɔrɛ nɛreba la, gee figehe zim i yi' la me bo' ba za'a.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Ti ba to'e tɔrɛ nɛreba la, ti ba za'a di tigɛ,

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: gee ti ba doohe va boroboro la, la zim la ti ba obe tigɛ gee bahɛ la, pirɛ pitɔ pia la tu yi'.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Bala n ba'ahɛ la, ti Yezu bahɛ ti a zahereba la zom uureŋɔ diŋɛ nɛŋa yakera ko'om la yagenɛ deyima ha, Bɛtesaada bɔba, gee ti a yee keehe nɛrekuuŋɔ la ba'ahɛ.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: En bahɛ ba ba'ahɛ la, a zom la zoore la zuo ti a ta pu'uhɛ.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Yinɛ n ta ma'ɛ la, ti a zahereba la pun bɔna uureŋɔ la puan mɛ yɛ'ɛha gee ti a ma'a yee bɔna doone.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Yezu n ta nyɛ ti uureŋɔ la mɔŋa kpe'em bo' a zahereba la, doohe la kusebego n lobere tu'uha ba la, ti a sige ko'om la zuo kina tu'uha ba na. Sankaŋa ti la kɔ'ɔm ita la funa funa wuu gogo muma anaahi bɔba la, ti a kina tɔɔra ba na wuu a yeti a zom la ba uureŋɔ la zuo la.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Gee ban nyɛ en ti a kina ko'om la zuo la, ti ba ti'ihɛ ti a dela kukɔ, ti ba kaahɛ.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Gee nyaa zom uureŋɔ la zuo la ba, ti kusebego la ma'aɛ, ti la iŋɛ ba za'a pakerɛ.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba pun ka bɔkɛ boroboro kirega la vuurɛ, gee me kãbɛ ba suya sina. Yezu tibɛ nɛreba Genesarete (Matu

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: ze'ele ku puan sige na la, ti nɛreba pun baŋɛ en kalam,

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: zɔta ɛɛra timpaberɛ la puan zi'an woo tara bã'duma n ga' ba sunɔ zuto, kina zi'an woo ti ba wum ti a boi bini.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ti zi'an woo ti Yezu kiŋɛ, tinkãra bii timbibehi puan, bii va'am puan, nɛreba ni tari la bã'duma yehe na beŋe da'ahi puan, gee belum en ti a bahɛ ti ba yeen kalum a fuo la timpaberɛ, ti se'em woo n ni kalum ni sirum nyɛ la tibegɔ.
